metadata.title: ICH Guidelines — Complete Reference Database for NIX AI
metadata.description: Comprehensive ICH guideline database covering Quality (Q), Safety (S), Efficacy (E), and Multidisciplinary (M) guidelines with detailed section references, requirements, and compliance criteria for clinical trial protocol analysis.

E1.title: The Extent of Population Exposure to Assess Clinical Safety
E1.focus: Safety database size requirements for marketing applications
key_requirements: For drugs intended for chronic use: minimum 1500 patients exposed, 300-600 for 6 months, 100 for 12 months
Database size justification based on intended patient population
Short-term exposure requirements vary by disease severity and treatment alternatives
Special populations (elderly, renal/hepatic impairment) should be adequately represented

sections.1: Introduction — rationale for safety database size
sections.2: Overall Safety Database — total exposure requirements
sections.2.1: Long-term treatment — chronic disease exposure minimums
sections.3: Special Situations — dose-response, pediatric, rare diseases

critical: Minimum 100 patients exposed for 12 months for chronic indications
Total database ≥1500 patients
minor: Dose-response data availability

E2A.title: Clinical Safety Data Management: Definitions and Standards for Expedited Reporting
E2A.focus: Definitions of adverse events, serious adverse events, and expedited reporting requirements
key_requirements: Clear definitions: Adverse Event (AE), Adverse Drug Reaction (ADR), Unexpected ADR, Serious AE
Expedited reporting timelines: fatal/life-threatening within 7 days, all serious within 15 days
Causality assessment methodology documentation
Investigator brochure update when new safety signals emerge
Blinding/unblinding procedures for safety reporting

sections.II: Definitions — AE, ADR, Unexpected ADR, Serious AE
sections.III: Standards for Expedited Reporting — timelines and criteria
sections.III.A: What should be reported: serious, unexpected ADRs
sections.III.C: Special situations: pregnancy, overdose, lack of efficacy
sections.IV: Other Safety Reports — periodic reports during development

critical: SAE definition aligned with ICH E2A
Expedited reporting timelines in protocol
DSMB/DMC charter for safety oversight
major: AE grading scale specified (CTCAE or equivalent)
minor: Adverse event collection period specified post-treatment

E2F.title: Development Safety Update Report
E2F.focus: Annual safety reporting during clinical development
key_requirements: Annual DSUR submission to all relevant regulatory authorities
Standard format and content requirements
Overall benefit-risk assessment based on cumulative safety data
Signal detection methodology
Reference safety information updates

sections.6: Summary of important risks and new safety information

critical: DSUR submission plan documented
Benefit-risk assessment framework defined
minor: DSUR submission timeline aligned with DIBD

E3.title: Structure and Content of Clinical Study Reports
E3.focus: Standard format for clinical study reports (CSRs)
key_requirements: 16-section standardized CSR structure
Synopsis requirements for all CSRs
Statistical analysis details and display conventions
Appendix requirements: protocol, SAP, CRF, patient data listings
Efficacy and safety data presentation standards

sections.11: Efficacy Evaluation — primary and secondary endpoints
sections.12: Safety Evaluation — AEs, laboratory, vital signs, ECG
sections.14: Tables, Figures, and Graphs (referenced in text)
sections.16: Appendices — Protocol, SAP, CRF, Patient Listings

critical: CSR structure follows ICH E3 format
minor: Data listing format standards

E4.title: Dose-Response Information to Support Drug Registration
E4.focus: Dose selection, dose-response studies, PK/PD relationships
key_requirements: Dose-response relationship characterization before Phase III
At least 3 dose levels recommended for dose-response studies
PK/PD modeling when direct dose-response data is limited
Dose adjustment criteria for special populations
Starting dose justification for first-in-human studies

critical: Dose selection rationale documented with PK/PD data or dose-response study
minor: Food effect assessment planned

E5_R1.title: Ethnic Factors in the Acceptability of Foreign Clinical Data
E5_R1.focus: Ethnic sensitivity assessment, bridging studies, foreign data acceptance
key_requirements: Ethnic factors classification: intrinsic (genetics, metabolism) and extrinsic (diet, culture, medical practice)
Bridging study design when ethnic sensitivity is a concern
Complete clinical data package for each region
Pharmacogenomic considerations
Dose adjustment based on ethnic PK/PD differences

critical: Ethnic sensitivity assessment for multi-regional trials
major: Bridging study plan for new regions
minor: Cultural adaptation of patient-reported outcomes

E6_R3.title: Good Clinical Practice
E6_R3.focus: Trial conduct, quality, data integrity, informed consent, risk-based approaches
key_requirements: Quality by Design (QbD) approach to clinical trials
Risk-based quality management system (RBQMS)
Proportionate approaches to source data verification
Enhanced informed consent requirements including digital consent
Investigator responsibilities and oversight
Sponsor responsibilities for trial quality
IRB/IEC requirements for protocol approval
Essential document management (TMF)
Data governance and integrity principles
Technology-enabled trial conduct
Decentralized clinical trial considerations

sections.2: Principles of ICH GCP — 13 fundamental principles
sections.5.0: Quality management — critical to quality factors
sections.5.2: Trial management, data handling, record keeping

critical: Informed consent process documented per Section 4.8
IRB/IEC approval obtained before enrollment
Risk-based quality management plan
Protocol includes all Section 6 elements
Investigator qualifications verified
Safety reporting procedures defined
minor: Trial Master File (TMF) management plan
Technology platforms for data capture specified

E7.title: Studies in Support of Special Populations: Geriatrics
E7.focus: Elderly patient inclusion, age-specific dosing, safety considerations
key_requirements: Patients ≥65 years should be included in clinical trials unless excluded by medical criteria
Minimum 100 geriatric patients in drugs for diseases of aging
PK studies in elderly to determine dose adjustments
Drug-drug interaction studies relevant to geriatric polypharmacy
Assessment of cognitive function, falls, renal function

critical: Elderly inclusion criteria justified
No arbitrary upper age limit without medical justification
minor: Geriatric-specific quality of life instruments considered

E8_R1.title: General Considerations for Clinical Studies
E8_R1.focus: Study design, quality factors, fit-for-purpose studies, innovative designs
key_requirements: Quality by Design approach to clinical studies
Critical to Quality (CtQ) factor identification
Fit-for-purpose study designs — match rigor to decision importance
Stakeholder engagement in study design
Proportionate approach to data collection
Study types: traditional, pragmatic, observational, decentralized

sections.2: Objectives and Design — study quality factors
sections.2.1: Study objectives should address specific questions
sections.2.3: Study design selection — fit for purpose

critical: Study objectives clearly defined
CtQ factors identified and managed
minor: Stakeholder engagement documentation

E9_R1.title: Statistical Principles for Clinical Trials — Addendum: Estimands and Sensitivity Analysis
E9_R1.focus: Estimands framework, intercurrent events, sensitivity analysis strategies
key_requirements: Estimand framework: 5 attributes (Population, Treatment, Variable, Intercurrent Events, Summary Measure)
Intercurrent event strategies: treatment policy, composite, hypothetical, principal stratum, while-on-treatment
Primary estimand aligned with study objective and clinical question
Sensitivity analyses to test robustness of primary analysis
Supplementary analyses for additional clinical questions
Missing data handling aligned with estimand strategy

sections.A.2: Estimand: A precise description of the treatment effect
sections.A.2.1: Attributes of an Estimand — the 5 components
sections.A.3: Impact on Trial Design and Conduct
sections.A.5: Documentation — estimand in protocol and SAP

critical: Primary estimand explicitly defined with all 5 attributes
Intercurrent events identified with handling strategies
SAP describes sensitivity analyses aligned with estimand
major: Missing data strategy aligned with estimand
Sample size justified for primary estimand
minor: Estimand framework training for study team documented

E10.title: Choice of Control Group and Related Issues in Clinical Trials
E10.focus: Active vs. placebo controls, non-inferiority, assay sensitivity
key_requirements: Control group selection rationale (placebo, active, dose-response, historical)
Ethical considerations for placebo use — Declaration of Helsinki compliance
Non-inferiority margin selection based on historical effect size
Assay sensitivity demonstration requirements
Superiority vs. non-inferiority vs. equivalence trial considerations

sections.2: Types of control groups — placebo, active, dose-response, historical, no treatment
sections.3: Choosing the control group — scientific and ethical considerations
sections.4: Non-inferiority trials — margin selection, constancy assumption
sections.5: Practical considerations — sample size, regional requirements

critical: Control group selection justified scientifically and ethically
Non-inferiority margin justified with historical data if applicable
Assay sensitivity addressed for non-inferiority trials
major: Placebo use ethically justified per Declaration of Helsinki
Active comparator reflects standard of care
minor: Rescue medication criteria specified

E11_R1.title: Clinical Investigation of Medicinal Products in the Pediatric Population
E11_R1.focus: Pediatric development, extrapolation, age-appropriate formulations, ethical consent
key_requirements: Pediatric Investigation Plan (PIP) / Pediatric Study Plan (PSP)
Age categories: preterm neonates, term neonates (0-27d), infants (28d-23m), children (2-11y), adolescents (12-17y)
Extrapolation framework: when adult data can support pediatric approvals
Age-appropriate formulations required
Assent requirements by age group
Modeling and simulation for dose selection
Long-term follow-up for growth and development

critical: Pediatric development plan documented (PIP/PSP)
Age-appropriate dose selection justified
Assent/consent procedures for each age group
major: PK/PD data collection in pediatric age groups
minor: Age-appropriate formulation development plan

E14.title: The Clinical Evaluation of QT/QTc Interval Prolongation and Proarrhythmic Potential for Non-Antiarrhythmic Drugs
E14.focus: Cardiac safety, thorough QT study (TQS), concentration-response (C-R) analysis
key_requirements: Thorough QT study (TQS) or concentration-response (C-R) analysis
Positive control (moxifloxacin) in TQS design
ECG collection methodology — triplicate, centralized reading
QTc correction formula selection (Fridericia preferred)
Exposure-response analysis integrating Phase I-III data
Threshold of regulatory concern: >10ms QTc prolongation

critical: Cardiac safety strategy defined (TQS or C-R approach)
ECG collection schedule specified in protocol
minor: Holter monitoring for drugs with arrhythmia signals

E17.title: General Principles for Planning and Design of Multi-Regional Clinical Trials
E17.focus: Global trial design, regional sample sizes, ethnic factors, consistency assessment
key_requirements: Multi-Regional Clinical Trial (MRCT) design principles
Regional sample size allocation methodology
Consistency assessment across regions — quantitative and qualitative
Intrinsic and extrinsic ethnic factors evaluation (per E5)
Regulatory alignment across participating regions
Poolability assessment for combining regional data
Pre-specified subgroup analyses by region

sections.2: Principles — MRCT as single trial vs. regional programs
sections.3.1: Overall sample size and regional allocation
sections.6: Regulatory submissions and use of MRCT data

critical: Regional sample size allocation plan documented
Consistency assessment methodology pre-specified
Regulatory alignment plan for all participating regions
minor: Cultural adaptation of PRO instruments

E19.title: Optimization of Safety Data Collection
E19.focus: Selective safety data collection in late-stage confirmatory trials
key_requirements: Selective safety data collection for well-characterized drugs in late-stage trials
Targeted AE collection based on known safety profile
Serious adverse event collection always required
Pre-specified safety data collection plan with justification
Not applicable to first-in-class drugs or new safety signals

critical: SAE collection plan maintained regardless of selective approach
major: Justification for selective AE collection based on prior data
minor: Safety data collection rationale documented in protocol

E20.title: Adaptive Clinical Trials
E20.focus: Adaptive designs, interim analyses, type I error control, operational bias control
key_requirements: Types of adaptations: sample size re-estimation, dose selection, population enrichment, endpoint modification
Type I error rate control across adaptations
Operational bias mitigation — blinding, pre-specification
Statistical framework: group sequential, adaptive seamless Phase II/III
Independent DMC for interim analyses
Simulation-based operating characteristics
Regulatory interaction recommended before implementation

critical: All adaptations pre-specified in protocol and SAP
Type I error control demonstrated via simulation
DMC charter for interim analysis decision-making
major: Blinding maintained during adaptations where possible
Decision rules for each adaptation pre-specified
minor: Regulatory consultation for novel adaptive designs

E22.title: General Considerations for Patient Preference Studies
E22.focus: Patient preference information, benefit-risk assessment, stated/revealed preferences
key_requirements: Patient preference studies as input to benefit-risk assessment
Stated preference methods: DCE, threshold technique, swing weighting
Revealed preference methods: treatment switching data
Study design and validation requirements
Integration with regulatory decision-making

critical: Patient preference methodology validated if used for regulatory decisions
major: Sample size justified for preference study
minor: Patient preference considered in protocol design

S1A.title: Need for Carcinogenicity Studies of Pharmaceuticals
S1A.focus: When carcinogenicity studies are required based on duration of use, genotoxicity signals, structural alerts
key_requirements: Carcinogenicity studies required for drugs used continuously for ≥6 months
Required for drugs with genotoxicity signals, structural alerts to known carcinogens
Not required for drugs used for short-term, life-threatening conditions
Two-year rodent bioassay typically required (rat; mouse may be replaced by alternative model)
Timing: completed before NDA/MAA filing or Phase III if patient population at risk

critical: Carcinogenicity study need assessment documented
Studies initiated before Phase III for drugs requiring them
major: Two-species study plan or justified alternative approach
Genotoxicity battery completed before carcinogenicity study design
minor: Carcinogenicity study timeline aligned with registration filing

S2_R1.title: Guidance on Genotoxicity Testing and Data Interpretation
S2_R1.focus: Standard genotoxicity battery: in vitro gene mutation, chromosomal damage, in vivo test
key_requirements: Standard battery: (1) Bacterial reverse mutation (Ames test), (2) In vitro mammalian cell assay (chromosomal aberration or mouse lymphoma), (3) In vivo test (micronucleus or chromosomal aberration)
Option 1 (preferred): Ames + in vitro + in vivo micronucleus
Option 2: Ames + two in vivo assays (micronucleus + comet or transgenic)
Positive genotoxicity results require weight-of-evidence assessment
Completed before Phase I for small molecules; before Phase III for biologics

critical: Complete genotoxicity battery before human exposure
Positive signals addressed with mechanistic follow-up studies
major: Standard battery completed per ICH S2(R1)
minor: Follow-up study rationale documented

S5_R3.title: Detection of Toxicity to Reproduction for Human Pharmaceuticals
S5_R3.focus: Fertility, embryo-fetal development, pre/postnatal development studies
key_requirements: Three-segment design: fertility (Segment I), embryo-fetal development (Segment II), pre/postnatal (Segment III)
EFD studies in two species (rat + rabbit) before enrollment of women of childbearing potential
Fertility studies before Phase III or NDA/MAA
R3 update: integrated study designs, reduced animal use, enhanced exposure assessment
Contraception requirements in clinical trials based on reproductive toxicity data

critical: EFD completed before WOCBP enrollment
Contraception requirements specified in protocol
major: Fertility and pre/postnatal studies planned per development timeline
Exposure margins calculated vs. clinical dose
minor: Juvenile animal study timeline for pediatric indications

S6_R1.title: Preclinical Safety Evaluation of Biotechnology-Derived Pharmaceuticals
S6_R1.focus: Biologics-specific nonclinical requirements: species selection, immunogenicity, reproductive toxicity
key_requirements: Relevant species selection based on pharmacological activity (not just binding)
One species may be sufficient if only one relevant species exists
Immunogenicity assessment: anti-drug antibodies (ADA) impact on toxicology interpretation
Reproductive toxicity: homologous protein or surrogate molecule approach
Carcinogenicity studies generally not required for biologics
Tissue cross-reactivity studies for monoclonal antibodies
Recovery groups included in pivotal studies

critical: Relevant species justified
Immunogenicity monitoring in tox studies
major: ADA impact on PK/PD and toxicity interpretation
minor: Tissue cross-reactivity completed for mAbs

S7B.title: Nonclinical Evaluation of the Potential for Delayed Ventricular Repolarization (QT Interval Prolongation)
S7B.focus: hERG assay, in vivo QT studies, integrated risk assessment with E14
key_requirements: hERG channel inhibition assay (in vitro)
In vivo QT study in conscious telemetered animals
Integrated risk assessment combining S7B and E14/clinical data
2020 Q&A update: allows in silico and in vitro approaches to replace in vivo QT study
Concentration-response analysis for hERG data
Safety margins: comparison of hERG IC50 to clinical Cmax

critical: hERG assay completed before first human dose
QT risk assessment documented
major: In vivo QT study or justified waiver
Integrated risk assessment per S7B/E14 Q&A
minor: Dose-dependent QTc relationship characterized

S9.title: Nonclinical Evaluation for Anticancer Pharmaceuticals
S9.focus: Reduced nonclinical requirements for life-threatening oncology indications
key_requirements: Reduced nonclinical package for anti-cancer drugs: life-threatening indications
Genotoxicity: Ames test sufficient before Phase I; full battery before Phase II/III
Carcinogenicity: generally not required for cytotoxic drugs
Reproductive toxicity: EFD in one species before Phase III; not required before Phase I/II
Safety pharmacology: can be assessed within general toxicology studies
Applies to drugs for advanced cancer; different requirements for adjuvant settings

critical: S9 applicability justified (advanced vs early stage cancer)
Minimum tox package for Phase I complete
major: EFD planning for Phase III aligned
Genotoxicity battery completed before late-stage trials
minor: Immunotoxicity assessment for immunomodulatory agents

S11.title: Nonclinical Safety Testing in Support of Development of Paediatric Pharmaceuticals
S11.focus: Juvenile animal studies, timing relative to pediatric clinical trials
key_requirements: Juvenile animal studies (JAS) to assess developmental toxicity
Species selection: rat or dog typically; species with similar postnatal development to humans
Timing: JAS before pediatric clinical trials; must cover age-relevant developmental stages
Not always required: consider existing adult data, mechanism of action, human pediatric data
Study design: age at treatment start based on target pediatric age, endpoints for growth and development
Decision framework for when JAS is needed vs. can be waived

critical: JAS need assessment documented
JAS completed before pediatric dosing if required
major: Species and age at dosing start justified
minor: JAS waiver rationale documented if not conducted

S12.title: Nonclinical Biodistribution Considerations for Gene Therapy Products
S12.focus: Gene therapy biodistribution study design and timing
key_requirements: Biodistribution assessment for gene therapy products using vector DNA/RNA by qPCR
Target and non-target tissue analysis including gonads for germline integration risk
Shedding studies for viral vector gene therapies
Integration analysis for integrating vectors (e.g., lentiviral)
Biodistribution data used to support first-in-human dose selection
Long-term follow-up considerations based on vector persistence

critical: Biodistribution study completed before first human dose
Gonadal distribution assessed
major: Shedding study plan for viral vectors
Integration site analysis for integrating vectors
minor: Long-term persistence follow-up plan

E2B_R3.title: Individual Case Safety Reports (ICSRs) — Electronic Transmission
E2B_R3.focus: Standardized electronic format for individual case safety report (ICSR) transmission
key_requirements: E2B(R3) XML format for ICSR transmission globally
MedDRA coding for adverse events and indications
Minimum data elements: identifiable reporter, identifiable patient, suspected product, adverse event
Transmission to EudraVigilance (EMA), FAERS (FDA), PMDA safety reporting system
Narrative section requirements for case description
Causality assessment per Naranjo or WHO-UMC system

critical: ICSR reporting system capability established
MedDRA coding system implemented
major: Reporter and patient identifiability criteria met
minor: Narrative quality standards defined

E2C_R2.title: Periodic Benefit-Risk Evaluation Report (PBRER)
E2C_R2.focus: Periodic safety reporting during post-approval and development lifecycle
key_requirements: Replaces PSUR (Periodic Safety Update Report) with enhanced benefit-risk evaluation
Data lock points aligned with International Birth Date (IBD)
Cumulative and interval safety data analysis
Benefit-risk evaluation framework per ICH E2C(R2) Section V
Signal detection and evaluation of new safety information
Risk management plan integration
Submission to all ICH regulatory authorities

critical: PBRER submission plan documented
Benefit-risk assessment methodology defined
minor: PBRER format aligned with ICH E2C(R2) template

E2D.title: Post-Approval Safety Data Management: Definitions and Standards for Expedited Reporting
E2D.focus: Post-marketing safety data collection, evaluation, and expedited reporting standards
key_requirements: Expedited reporting of serious unexpected ADRs from any source (spontaneous, literature, studies)
15-day reporting for all serious unexpected post-marketing ADRs
7-day reporting for fatal or life-threatening ADRs
Literature monitoring for safety signals
Stimulated reporting vs. spontaneous reporting distinction
Minimum criteria for valid ICSR in post-marketing setting

critical: Post-marketing safety reporting procedures established
Expedited reporting timelines defined
minor: Reporting criteria documented for different ADR sources

E2E.title: Pharmacovigilance Planning
E2E.focus: Safety specification and pharmacovigilance plan development for regulatory submission
key_requirements: Safety specification summarising known and potential risks, missing information
Pharmacovigilance plan addressing safety concerns
Risk management measures: routine and additional
Integration with risk management plan (RMP for EMA) or REMS (FDA)
Post-marketing study commitments for safety monitoring

critical: Safety specification developed based on pre-approval data
Pharmacovigilance plan submitted with registration application
major: Key safety concerns identified and mitigation planned
minor: Routine pharmacovigilance measures documented

E12.title: Clinical Evaluation by Therapeutic Category — Principles
E12.focus: General principles for clinical evaluation structured by therapeutic category
key_requirements: Dose-finding rationale per therapeutic category
Control group selection adapted to disease area
Endpoint selection relevant to therapeutic area
Safety database size guidance by therapeutic category
Cross-reference to therapeutic area-specific annexes

E15.title: Definitions for Genomic Biomarkers, Pharmacogenomics, Pharmacogenetics, Genomic Data and Sample Coding Categories
E15.focus: Standardized terminology for pharmacogenomics in drug development
key_requirements: Definitions: genomic biomarker, pharmacogenomics, pharmacogenetics, DNA/RNA sample categories
Coding categories for genomic data: identified, coded, anonymized
Framework for genomic biomarker use in clinical trials
Consent requirements for genomic sample collection

E16.title: Biomarkers Related to Drug or Biotechnology Product Development: Context, Structure, and Format of Qualification Submissions
E16.focus: Biomarker qualification process for regulatory acceptance across ICH regions
key_requirements: Context of Use (COU) definition for biomarker qualification
Submission format for biomarker qualification: rationale, evidence, COU statement
Evidentiary standards for biomarker validation
Biomarker qualification applicable to safety and efficacy biomarkers
Accepted qualifications applicable across ICH regions

E18.title: Genomic Sampling and Management of Genomic Data
E18.focus: Practical guidance on genomic sample collection, storage, and data management in clinical trials
key_requirements: Informed consent for genomic sampling: specific, broad, or tiered consent models
Sample collection: timing, handling, storage, and shipping requirements
Data management: privacy, coding, access controls, data sharing
Biobank governance and sample retention policies
Pharmacogenomic data reporting in regulatory submissions

Q1A_R2.title: Stability Testing of New Drug Substances and Products
Q1A_R2.focus: Stability study design, storage conditions, shelf life determination
key_requirements: Long-term stability: 25°C/60% RH for 12 months minimum at submission
Accelerated stability: 40°C/75% RH for 6 months
Photostability per ICH Q1B
Stress testing for degradation pathway identification
Stability-indicating analytical methods required
Statistical analysis of stability data for shelf life extrapolation

critical: Long-term and accelerated stability data available at filing
minor: Stress testing completed for degradation pathway understanding

Q5E.title: Comparability of Biotechnological/Biological Products
Q5E.focus: Comparability assessment for manufacturing changes to biologics
key_requirements: Comparability exercise required for manufacturing process changes
Analytical comparability: head-to-head comparison of quality attributes
Non-clinical and clinical comparability may be required depending on change magnitude
Risk-based approach to determine extent of comparability studies needed
Applicable to all biotechnology-derived products (mAbs, proteins, vaccines)

critical: Comparability protocol defined for planned manufacturing changes
minor: Comparability assessment strategy aligned with product lifecycle

Q8_R2.title: Pharmaceutical Development
Q8_R2.focus: Quality by Design, design space, critical quality attributes
key_requirements: Quality by Design (QbD) approach: systematic development based on sound science
Quality Target Product Profile (QTPP) definition
Critical Quality Attributes (CQAs) identification and justification
Design space establishment through multivariate experiments
Control strategy development based on product and process understanding
Real-time release testing and PAT (Process Analytical Technology)

critical: CQAs identified and justified
Control strategy defined
minor: QbD principles applied in formulation development

Q9_R1.title: Quality Risk Management
Q9_R1.focus: Risk assessment tools, FMEA, HACCP for pharma manufacturing
key_requirements: Quality risk management principles throughout product lifecycle
Risk assessment tools: FMEA, FMECA, HACCP, FTA, HAZOP
Risk identification, analysis, evaluation, control, review cycle
R1 update: addresses subjectivity in risk assessment, formalised risk management
Integration with pharmaceutical quality system (ICH Q10)
Application to GMP, regulatory decisions, and development activities

critical: Risk management methodology documented and applied
major: Risk tools appropriate to complexity of decision
Risk review and communication processes established
minor: Risk management training for relevant personnel

Q10.title: Pharmaceutical Quality System
Q10.focus: Lifecycle approach to GMP, knowledge management, continual improvement
key_requirements: Pharmaceutical Quality System (PQS) across product lifecycle
Management responsibility and commitment
Knowledge management: systematic approach to acquiring and sharing knowledge
Continual improvement through CAPA, change management, management review
Process performance and product quality monitoring
Integration with GMP and ICH Q8/Q9

critical: PQS documented and implemented
minor: Management review conducted periodically

Q12.title: Technical and Regulatory Considerations for Pharmaceutical Product Lifecycle Management
Q12.focus: Post-approval changes, established conditions, regulatory flexibility
key_requirements: Established Conditions (ECs): elements requiring regulatory notification for changes
Post-Approval Change Management Protocols (PACMPs)
Product Lifecycle Management (PLCM) approach
Categorisation of post-approval changes based on risk
Regulatory flexibility for well-understood products
Knowledge management for lifecycle decisions

critical: ECs identified in registration dossier
major: Change management protocol for anticipated changes
minor: PLCM approach documented

M4.title: Common Technical Document (CTD)
key_requirements: Module 1: Administrative Information (region-specific)
Module 2: Quality Overall Summary, Non-clinical Overview, Clinical Overview
Module 3: Quality (CMC data)
Module 4: Non-clinical Study Reports
Module 5: Clinical Study Reports
eCTD electronic submission format required by all major regulators

critical: CTD structure followed for all submissions
minor: Hyperlinks and cross-references functional in eCTD

M4E_R2.title: CTD — Efficacy
M4E_R2.focus: Module 5 clinical overview, clinical summary, tabular summaries
key_requirements: Module 2.5: Clinical Overview — integrated benefit-risk assessment
Module 2.7: Clinical Summary — biopharmaceutics, PK, efficacy, safety tabular summaries
Module 5.3: Clinical Study Reports organized by type
Tabular format for demographic, efficacy, and safety summaries
Individual patient data listings in appendices

M7_R2.title: Assessment and Control of DNA Reactive Impurities
M7_R2.focus: Mutagenic impurity risk assessment, TTC, Ames test data
key_requirements: Risk assessment framework for mutagenic impurities in drug substances and products
Threshold of Toxicological Concern (TTC): 1.5 μg/day lifetime intake limit
Less-than-lifetime exposure limits: higher limits for shorter durations
In silico and Ames test for mutagenicity assessment
Control strategies: avoid, control, or purge-based approaches
Cohort of Concern: specific structural alerts requiring lower limits

critical: Mutagenic impurity risk assessment completed before Phase I
major: TTC-based limits applied or justified alternatives
minor: In silico assessment tools validated and documented

M11.title: Clinical Electronic Structured Harmonised Protocol (CeSHarP)
M11.focus: Standardized electronic protocol template and structure
key_requirements: Common protocol template structure for all ICH regions
Machine-readable protocol elements for automated processing

Standard sections: Title Page, Synopsis, Schedule of Activities, Introduction, Objectives/Estimands, Study Design, Study Population, Study Intervention, Assessments, Statistical Considerations, Oversight
Harmonized terminology and definitions across ICH regions
Digital protocol enabling automated regulatory review
Protocol amendments structured consistently
Alignment with CDISC standards for data collection

sections.4: Introduction — Disease Background, Study Rationale, Benefit-Risk
sections.6: Study Design — description, schematic, scientific rationale
sections.7: Study Population — eligibility criteria, lifestyle restrictions
sections.8: Study Intervention — investigational product, concomitant therapy

critical: Protocol follows M11 section structure
Estimand framework included in objectives section
major: Schedule of Activities complete and consistent
minor: CDISC alignment for data collection elements

M14.title: General Principles on Real World Data/Evidence
M14.focus: Use of real-world data and real-world evidence in regulatory decisions
key_requirements: Definitions: Real-World Data (RWD), Real-World Evidence (RWE)
RWD sources: electronic health records, claims data, registries, wearables, patient-generated data
Fitness-for-purpose assessment of RWD for regulatory use
Data quality requirements: completeness, accuracy, timeliness, relevance
Study design considerations for RWE generation: target trial emulation, pragmatic trials
Regulatory applications: new indications, safety monitoring, effectiveness confirmation
Governance, data access, and privacy considerations

critical: RWD fitness-for-purpose assessment documented if RWE used
major: Study design aligned with regulatory question
minor: RWD governance and privacy measures documented
